new_classes.label_en: PDO Still Wine in Containers of 2 Litres or Less (CN 22042168)
new_classes.label_de: Stillwein mit g.U. in Behältnissen bis 2 Liter (KN 22042168)
new_classes.definition_en: Non-sparkling wine of fresh grapes with a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) as defined in Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22, presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres; classified under CN code 22042168.

A quality that attaches to a wine when its production, processing and preparation take place in a defined geographical area with recognised know-how, as recognised under EU law and registered as a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO / g.U.) pursuant to Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22 of the CN.

coverage_explanation: The three principal discriminating criteria are captured: (1) the wine is non-sparkling (StillWine parent), (2) it carries a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO/g.U.) via the new ProtectedDesignationOfOrigin quality and the someValuesFrom restriction on hasGeographicOriginQuality, and (3) it is presented in containers not exceeding 2 litres (decimalRange on maxContainerVolumeL). Coverage is reduced to 0.75 because the detailed substance of Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22 — which elaborates the precise legal conditions defining a PDO for wine (geographical area, production rules, registration requirements) — is referenced but not itself formalised as further OWL axioms; those conditions are purely normative/legal text and cannot be fully expressed in OWL-DL without additional fact data. Additionally, the sibling CN codes 22042111–22042178 that share the same PDO range are not explicitly discriminated from 22042168 by further alcohol-by-volume or colour sub-criteria (which would require additional legal notes not provided here).
